title: SQLite Concurrent Write Stress Test (100 Writers, 60s)

observations: WAL mode enabled with NORMAL synchronous - good balance of safety/performance
No SQLITE_BUSY errors - busy_timeout of 5000ms was sufficient
308 lock errors (0.57% error rate) - acceptable under extreme contention
p50 latency 1ms is excellent; p99 at 2.8s indicates tail latency from lock contention
Integrity check passed - no data corruption under stress
recommendations: For lower p99 latency, consider reducing writer count or using connection pooling
Current settings appropriate for moderate-to-high concurrent write workloads
Lock errors are transient and handled by retry - not a concern at 0.57% rate

cross_lane_hooks.research: Validates concurrent DB access patterns for production workloads
cross_lane_hooks.ops: Baseline performance data for capacity planning
